name: CustomRegistry
summary: Abstract base class for all scoped registries, standardizing API for storing and retrieving definitions with inheritance support.

name: HTMLRegistry
summary: Specialized abstract registry for storing constructors bound to DOM nodes, handling node upgrade and downgrade lifecycles.

name: CustomElementRegistry
summary: Replaces native window.customElements with an extended version supporting scoped registries and inheritance.

name: InjectorRoot
summary: Root container for dependency injection, handling the map of all active injectors in the DOM tree.

name: HTMLInjector
summary: Injector instance bound to a specific DOM node, providing hierarchical dependency resolution.

name: ModuleInjector
summary: Module-scoped injector for ES Module dependency injection, where any consumer can access providers since module scope is not spatially constrained.

name: InjectableModule
summary: Ergonomic facade wrapping a ModuleInjector with provide/consume/register methods and a bootstrap lifecycle for module-scoped dependency injection.

name: Node
summary: Adds injector hierarchy methods to Node prototype: getClosestInjector(), getOwnInjector(), hasOwnInjector(), injectors().

name: Element.innerHTML
summary: Intercepts innerHTML setter to propagate injector scope to newly created nodes.

name: CustomContextRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customContextTypes for registering context definitions that provide hierarchical data flow.

name: CustomContext
summary: Base class for defining context providers with lifecycle callbacks and consumption tracking.

name: Node
summary: Adds context methods to Node prototype: createContext(), getContext(), ensureContext(), getOwnContext(), hasContext(), hasOwnContext(), queryContext().

name: CustomElement
summary: Base class for custom elements with enhanced lifecycle and options support.

name: Node.cloneNode
summary: Patches cloneNode to preserve Custom Elements, Custom Comments, and Custom Text Nodes with their prototypes and options.

name: Element
summary: Patches DOM insertion methods (append, prepend, after, before, replaceChildren, replaceWith) for lazy element upgrade.

name: HTMLElement Constructors
summary: Wraps native HTMLElement constructors to enable the dynamic registry system.

name: Document.createElement
summary: Patches createElement to apply correct prototypes from the patched constructors.

name: DocumentFragment.ownerTemplate
summary: Adds ownerTemplate property to DocumentFragment for template ownership tracking.

name: HTMLTemplateElement.content
summary: Patches the content getter to support enhanced template functionality.

name: CustomAttributesRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customAttributes, monitoring DOM for attribute changes and facilitating upgrade of attributes into behaviors.

name: CustomAttribute
summary: Base class for creating behaviors, similar to HTMLElement, with lifecycle callbacks for attribute-based components.

name: CustomAttributeParserRegistry
summary: Allows defining parsing logic for attribute values, enabling custom syntax and transformations.

name: CustomCommentRegistry
summary: Enables attaching behavior to comment nodes, useful for structural directives like virtual elements.

name: CustomComment
summary: Base class for enhanced comments with lifecycle callbacks for comment-based directives.

name: CustomCommentParserRegistry
summary: Parses comment content to identify and upgrade custom comments into active directives.

name: CustomTemplateDirective
summary: Base class for template directives that control template instantiation and projection.

name: CustomTextNodeRegistry
summary: Allows defining parsers for text content, enabling custom template syntax like Mustache-style interpolation.

name: CustomTextNode
summary: Base class for enhanced text nodes with reactive binding capabilities.

name: CustomTextNodeParserRegistry
summary: Manages parsers that can interpret text node content and transform it into reactive components.

name: CustomTextNodeParser
summary: Abstract base class for text node parsers that detect expression syntax and split text into static and expression segments.

name: CustomExpressionParserRegistry
summary: Registry for expression parsers, standardizing how strings are parsed into executable expressions.

name: CustomExpressionParser
summary: Standardizes how strings are parsed into executable expressions for use in data bindings.

name: CustomScriptTypesRegistry
summary: Allows defining custom behavior for script tags with custom type attributes, enabling declarative scripting patterns.

name: CustomScriptType
summary: Base class extending HTMLScriptElement with lifecycle callbacks for custom script types.

name: CustomStoreRegistry
summary: Registry for state management stores, enabling reactive data flow patterns.

name: CustomPositioningRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customPositioning for registering named positioning providers (native CSS Anchor, Floating UI, …) so any component delegates anchoring to one shared engine resolved through the injector chain.

name: CustomPositioner
summary: Base contract a positioning provider implements — position(anchor, anchored, options) returning a cleanup. Satisfied alike by the native-first default and by every library adapter.

name: CustomRecoveryHandlerRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customRecovery for registering named recovery handlers tried in priority order — the first handler whose tryRecover() returns a non-null result owns the recovery. Any system (Resource Loader, form submission, background task) resolves the registry and delegates failure handling without knowing which handlers are registered.

name: CustomRecoveryHandler
summary: Base contract a recovery handler implements — tryRecover(error, context) returning a RecoveryResult or null. Returning null passes control to the next handler in registration order. Implementations include HTTP retry handlers, circuit breakers, offline queues, and custom domain handlers.

name: CustomTranslationProviderRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customTranslations for registering named translation providers tried in priority order — the first provider whose resolve(key, locale) returns a non-null MessageFn owns the message. Any consumer resolves the registry through the injector chain and reads messages via the @t context without knowing which provider answered or which library backs it.

name: CustomTranslationProvider
summary: Base contract a translation provider implements — resolve(key, locale) returning a MessageFn or null. Returning null declines the key and passes control down the locale fallback chain and the next registered provider. Implementations include a native Intl MF2 formatter, lazy-namespace catalogs, and library adapters (FormatJS, i18next, Lingui, Paraglide).

name: CustomValidityMergeRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customValidityMerge for registering named validity-merge strategies — how a control reduces several independent SourceResults (native/schema/async/manual) into one MergedValidity. The #004 OP-1 ruling fixes the MergedValidity surface, not the merge math: source-reduction (native-first default) and last-write-wins ship registered, custom strategies are first-class, and the orchestrator auto-stamps generation tokens so devs never hand-author ids.

name: CustomValidityMergeStrategy
summary: Base contract a merge strategy implements — merge(sources) returning a MergedValidity (the #004 OP-1 surface: state, valid/pending flags, ordered messages with sources, blocking source, generation token). Strategies vary the computation, never the surface — the only constraint that keeps them L1-swappable. Satisfied alike by the source-reduction default, the degenerate last-write-wins, and custom strategies.

name: CustomValidatorResolutionRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customValidatorResolution for registering named async-validator-resolution strategies — how an in-flight async check is reconciled when the input keeps changing. The named sibling of the validity-merge plane (#212): versioning (native-first default — stamp a per-field generation token, drop answers older than the current input) and cancellation (abort the in-flight request via AbortController) ship registered, custom strategies are first-class, and the async runner emits the same pending{version} → valid|invalid SourceResult the merge plane consumes.

name: CustomValidatorResolution
summary: Base contract an async-resolution strategy implements — startValidation(fieldId, input) opening a generation handle, shouldApplyResult(handle, result) deciding whether a freshly-arrived answer is still current (the heart of stale-drop), and onInputChange(fieldId, newInput) superseding/aborting an in-flight check. The only constraint is that results feed the same pending{version} → valid|invalid SourceResult #212 consumes. Satisfied alike by the versioning default, cancellation, and custom strategies.

name: CustomEditorEngineRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customEditorEngine for registering named editing-engine providers (native contenteditable+InputEvent, ProseMirror, Lexical, Slate, Quill) so any editing surface delegates to one shared engine resolved through the injector chain. The native-first default is registered by the standard; library adapters register on top, precedence + graceful degradation the only rules.

name: CustomEditorEngine
summary: Base contract an editing-engine provider implements — attach(host, options) wiring an editable surface over the structured-node-tree pivot and returning a cleanup, emitting normalized InputEvent-shaped changes and negotiating the available editing-surface capability tier (contenteditable floor, editcontext upgrade). Satisfied alike by the native-first contenteditable default and by every library adapter.

name: CustomSerializerRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customSerializer for registering named serializer providers that round-trip the structured content model to and from a wire format. Default-less core (the Config-Extends-Platform-Default shape, #590 Fork 6): a project config selects the platform default — the HTML flavor — while the registry itself ships no opinion, so any editor delegates serialization to one shared, swappable engine resolved through the injector chain.

name: CustomSerializer
summary: Base contract a serializer provider implements — serialize(model) → string and deserialize(string) → model — round-tripping the structured content pivot to a wire format. Satisfied alike by the HTML flavor default (the platform default the project config extends) and by every alternative flavor (Markdown, JSON node-tree, …).

name: CustomSanitizerRegistry
summary: Exposes window.customSanitizer for registering named sanitizer providers that strip untrusted markup to an allowed schema. Native Element.setHTML is the default (the platform floor), with a DOMPurify adapter as the production floor for engines that lack it (#590 Fork 7); composed by the editor on the insertFromPaste path. Sanitizer ownership is webvalidation (ratified default), consumed by webediting as a no-leakage client.

name: CustomSanitizer
summary: Base contract a sanitizer provider implements — sanitize(html, config) → safe-html — narrowing untrusted markup to an allowed element/attribute schema. Satisfied alike by the native Element.setHTML default and by the DOMPurify production-floor adapter.
